title: ARRIVAL Protocol: Structured Semantic Coordination Enables Sustainable Cross-Architecture AI Cooperation
description: Source code, experimental data, and documentation for the ARRIVAL Protocol — a structured multi-agent coordination framework enabling cross-architecture LLM ensembles to solve graduate-level science, practical tasks, and game-theoretic cooperation problems. Includes 2,200+ experiments across 23 phases and 17 LLM architectures, MEANING-CRDT v1.1 mathematical framework (11 theorems), Confidence-Gated Debate (CGD), 7 echo-chamber metrics, adversarial robustness testing, GovSim cooperation validation, memory persistence (ARRIVAL-MNEMO), and AutoGen (AG2) integration. Total experimental cost: under $95.
notes: Documentation is bilingual (English/Russian). Code is licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later; documentation under CC BY-NC 4.0. Mathematical foundation: MEANING-CRDT v1.1 (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18702383). Repository: https://github.com/mifkilla/Arrival-Protocol
